twitter.post: Tired of scrolling through endless articles to find one specific answer? You can ask our AI search anything about the news and get a direct answer with links to the sources. It is a much faster way to stay informed. HEADLINNE.com #news #tech

linkedin.title: Algorithms get a bad reputation for just showing us more of the same.
linkedin.body:
It is a common frustration. You click on one article about electric cars and suddenly your feed is nothing but car reviews for weeks. That is not personalization. That is just repeating the last thing you did.

We think a good news engine should do the opposite. It should treat your interests like a conversation, not a search history. When we built the technology behind Headlinne, we focused on how people actually read. You might be interested in climate change, but you also want to know about local politics and the latest tech trends. Your curiosity is broad, and your news feed should reflect that.

Instead of guessing what you want based on a single click, our system looks for the nuance in what you actually engage with over time. It learns that you might like deep dives on weekends but prefer quick summaries on Tuesday mornings. It is about respecting your time and your intelligence.

Technology is at its best when it gets out of the way and helps you discover something new, rather than trapping you in a bubble.
linkedin.cta: You can see how we are rethinking the news feed at HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: OpenAI safety lead steps down
slides.explanation: Johannes Heidecke is leaving OpenAI. This change happens as the company works to combine its research and safety teams more closely.

slides.headline: Apple takes OpenAI to court
slides.explanation: Apple claims OpenAI stole hardware secrets. They allege that former employees were encouraged to take confidential presentations and prototypes to their new jobs.

slides.headline: Paramount and Warner Bros. deal moves forward
slides.explanation: Oregon's Attorney General dropped his request to pause the merger. He had previously asked for a delay to review documents related to the takeover.

slides.headline: New scrutiny for Homeland Security
slides.explanation: Federal agents under the current administration are facing criticism. Recent reports highlight questions about the use of force and how agents conduct home visits.

slides.headline: ChatGPT wants to join your family
slides.explanation: OpenAI is hiring a manager to build tools for families and older adults. They want to make the app more useful for people at home.

instagram.caption: It is a busy day in tech with major leadership changes, legal battles, and shifting strategies for AI. We are breaking down the biggest headlines you need to know right now. Tap through to catch up on the stories that matter. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: Trump admin subpoenas NYT reporters
slides.explanation: Journalists are facing legal action after reporting on security concerns regarding the new Air Force One. The plane was a gift from Qatar.

slides.headline: Hunter Biden wins defamation lawsuit
slides.explanation: A judge ordered former Overstock CEO Patrick Byrne to pay $1.7 million. Byrne had falsely claimed Biden sought a bribe to influence policy on Iran assets.

slides.headline: Boat accident in Vietnam leaves 15 dead
slides.explanation: A speedboat capsized in rough seas near a southern Vietnam island. While 15 people died, local reports confirm 21 others were rescued.

slides.headline: Neon picks up OpenAI-focused film
slides.explanation: The film Artificial, which focuses on OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, has a new distributor. Amazon previously dropped the project, but now Neon will release it.

slides.headline: Pianist loses case against Melbourne orchestra
slides.explanation: A federal court ruled against Jayson Gillham in his unfair dismissal case. The court found the orchestra did not fire him unfairly over his comments about the war in Gaza.

instagram.caption: Catch up on today's top stories from around the globe. From major legal battles to tragic accidents, we have the facts you need to stay informed. Tap through to see what is happening in the world today. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
